metadata.name: pinterest_ads__ad_group_report
metadata.comment: Each record in this table represents the daily performance of ads at the campaign, advertiser, and ad group level.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: The performance date of the record.

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

campaign_name.name: campaign_name
campaign_name.comment: The name of the related Campaign.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: The ID of the related Campaign.

ad_group_name.name: ad_group_name
ad_group_name.comment: The name of the related Ad group.

ad_group_id.name: ad_group_id
ad_group_id.comment: The ID of the related Ad group.

spend.name: spend
spend.comment: The amount of spend that occurred on the day of the record.

clicks.name: clicks
clicks.comment: The number of paid and earned clicks that occurred on the day of the record.

impressions.name: impressions
impressions.comment: The number of paid and earned impressions that occurred on the day of the record.

total_conversions.name: total_conversions
total_conversions.comment: This is the sum of all website conversions, otherwise known as the number of conversion events. For example, if you track sign-ups and checkouts on your website, it's the sum of all sign-ups and checkouts attributed from clicks, engagements, and views on Pinterest. Example: If a user clicks on a Pinterest ad and completes two different conversion actions (e.g., signs up and checks out), this would be counted as 2 total conversions.

total_conversions_quantity.name: total_conversions_quantity
total_conversions_quantity.comment: Refers to the total count of items or units involved in the conversions. This metric is often more granular and relates to the specific quantity of products or services purchased or actions taken. Example: If a single checkout includes three items, the total_conversions_quantity would be 3, even though it counts as one total_conversion.

total_conversions_value.name: total_conversions_value
total_conversions_value.comment: Total conversions order value. The total value of the conversions, calculated by summing up the revenue or order values attributed to conversions. Converted from the source `total_conversions_value_in_micro_dollar` field.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: pinterest_ads__advertiser_report

source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

date_day.name: date_day

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

currency_code.name: currency_code
currency_code.comment: The currency code which the advertiser is set up using.

country.name: country
country.comment: The country code where the advertiser is located.

spend.name: spend
spend.comment: The amount of spend that occurred on the day of the record.

clicks.name: clicks
clicks.comment: The number of paid and earned clicks that occurred on the day of the record.

impressions.name: impressions
impressions.comment: The number of paid and earned impressions that occurred on the day of the record.

total_conversions.name: total_conversions
total_conversions.comment: This is the sum of all website conversions, otherwise known as the number of conversion events. For example, if you track sign-ups and checkouts on your website, it's the sum of all sign-ups and checkouts attributed from clicks, engagements, and views on Pinterest. Example: If a user clicks on a Pinterest ad and completes two different conversion actions (e.g., signs up and checks out), this would be counted as 2 total conversions.

total_conversions_quantity.name: total_conversions_quantity
total_conversions_quantity.comment: Refers to the total count of items or units involved in the conversions. This metric is often more granular and relates to the specific quantity of products or services purchased or actions taken. Example: If a single checkout includes three items, the total_conversions_quantity would be 3, even though it counts as one total_conversion.

total_conversions_value.name: total_conversions_value
total_conversions_value.comment: Total conversions order value. The total value of the conversions, calculated by summing up the revenue or order values attributed to conversions. Converted from the source `total_conversions_value_in_micro_dollar` field.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: pinterest_ads__campaign_country_report
metadata.comment: Each record in this table represents the daily performance of ads at the country and campaign level.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: The performance date of the record.

country_id.name: country_id
country_id.comment: The ID of the targeted country.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: The ID of the related Campaign.

spend.name: spend
spend.comment: The amount of spend that occurred on the day of the record.

clicks.name: clicks
clicks.comment: The number of paid and earned clicks that occurred on the day of the record.

impressions.name: impressions
impressions.comment: The number of paid and earned impressions that occurred on the day of the record.

total_conversions.name: total_conversions
total_conversions.comment: This is the sum of all website conversions, otherwise known as the number of conversion events. For example, if you track sign-ups and checkouts on your website, it's the sum of all sign-ups and checkouts attributed from clicks, engagements, and views on Pinterest. Example: If a user clicks on a Pinterest ad and completes two different conversion actions (e.g., signs up and checks out), this would be counted as 2 total conversions.

total_conversions_quantity.name: total_conversions_quantity
total_conversions_quantity.comment: Refers to the total count of items or units involved in the conversions. This metric is often more granular and relates to the specific quantity of products or services purchased or actions taken. Example: If a single checkout includes three items, the total_conversions_quantity would be 3, even though it counts as one total_conversion.

total_conversions_value.name: total_conversions_value
total_conversions_value.comment: Total conversions order value. The total value of the conversions, calculated by summing up the revenue or order values attributed to conversions. Converted from the source `total_conversions_value_in_micro_dollar` field.

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

campaign_name.name: campaign_name
campaign_name.comment: The name of the related Campaign.

budget_spend_cap.name: budget_spend_cap
budget_spend_cap.comment: Maximum amount allowed to be spent over a specified budget period for the campaign.

lifetime_spend_cap.name: lifetime_spend_cap
lifetime_spend_cap.comment: Total maximum spend allowed across the entire lifespan of the campaign.

default_ad_group_budget_in_micro_currenc.name: default_ad_group_budget_in_micro_currency
default_ad_group_budget_in_micro_currenc.comment: When transitioning from campaign budget optimization to non-campaign budget optimization, the default_ad_group_budget_in_micro_currency will propagate to each child ad groups daily budget. Unit is micro currency of the associated advertiser account.

campaign_end_time.name: campaign_end_time
campaign_end_time.comment: Timestamp when the campaign ended or is scheduled to end.

is_campaign_budget_optimization.name: is_campaign_budget_optimization
is_campaign_budget_optimization.comment: Determines if a campaign automatically generate ad-group level budgets given a campaign budget to maximize campaign outcome. When transitioning from non-cbo to cbo, all previous child ad group budget will be cleared.

is_flexible_daily_budgets.name: is_flexible_daily_budgets
is_flexible_daily_budgets.comment: Determine if a campaign has flexible daily budgets setup.

campaign_objective_type.name: campaign_objective_type
campaign_objective_type.comment: The primary goal of the campaign (e.g., brand awareness, conversions, video views).

campaign_start_time.name: campaign_start_time
campaign_start_time.comment: Timestamp when the campaign became active or was scheduled to start.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: pinterest_ads__pin_promotion_report
metadata.comment: Each record in this table represents the daily performance of ads at the advertiser, campaign, ad group, and pin level.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: The performance date of the record.

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: The ID of the related Campaign.

ad_group_id.name: ad_group_id
ad_group_id.comment: The ID of the related Ad group.

creative_type.name: creative_type
creative_type.comment:
The creative type. 
One of "APP", "APP_VIDEO", "BOARD", "CAROUSEL", "CINEMATIC", "COMMERCE", "MAX_VIDEO", "NATIVE_VIDEO", "REGULAR", 
"SEARCH_PROMINENCE", "SEARCH_PROMINENCE_CAROUSEL", "SHOPPING", "SHOP_THE_PIN", "THIRD_PARTY", or "VIDEO".

pin_promotion_id.name: pin_promotion_id
pin_promotion_id.comment: The ID of the related Pin promotion.

base_url.name: base_url
base_url.comment: The base URL of the ad, extracted from the `destination_url`.

spend.name: spend
spend.comment: The amount of spend that occurred on the day of the record.

clicks.name: clicks
clicks.comment: The number of paid and earned clicks that occurred on the day of the record.

impressions.name: impressions
impressions.comment: The number of paid and earned impressions that occurred on the day of the record.

total_conversions.name: total_conversions
total_conversions.comment: This is the sum of all website conversions, otherwise known as the number of conversion events. For example, if you track sign-ups and checkouts on your website, it's the sum of all sign-ups and checkouts attributed from clicks, engagements, and views on Pinterest. Example: If a user clicks on a Pinterest ad and completes two different conversion actions (e.g., signs up and checks out), this would be counted as 2 total conversions.

total_conversions_quantity.name: total_conversions_quantity
total_conversions_quantity.comment: Refers to the total count of items or units involved in the conversions. This metric is often more granular and relates to the specific quantity of products or services purchased or actions taken. Example: If a single checkout includes three items, the total_conversions_quantity would be 3, even though it counts as one total_conversion.

total_conversions_value.name: total_conversions_value
total_conversions_value.comment: Total conversions order value. The total value of the conversions, calculated by summing up the revenue or order values attributed to conversions. Converted from the source `total_conversions_value_in_micro_dollar` field.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_pinterest_ads__ad_group_history
metadata.comment: Each record represents a version of a Pinterest Ad Group.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

ad_group_status.name: ad_group_status
ad_group_status.comment: The status of the Ad group. One of "ACTIVE", "ARCHIVED", "PAUSED"

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when a record was last synced.

pacing_delivery_type.name: pacing_delivery_type
pacing_delivery_type.comment: Ad group pacing delivery type. With ACCELERATED, an ad group budget is spent as fast as possible. With STANDARD, an ad group budget is spent smoothly over a day. When using CBO, only the STANDARD pacing delivery type is allowed.

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Boolean representing whether the record is the most recent version of the object.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_pinterest_ads__advertiser_history
metadata.comment: Each record represents a version of an advertiser.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

country.name: country
country.comment: The country code where the advertiser is located.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Timestamp of when a record was created.

currency_code.name: currency_code
currency_code.comment: The currency code which the advertiser is set up using.

owner_user_id.name: owner_user_id
owner_user_id.comment: Unique identifier of the owner user.

advertiser_permissions.name: advertiser_permissions
advertiser_permissions.comment: The permissions associated with this account.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: Timestamp of when a record was last updated.

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Boolean representing whether the record is the most recent version of the object.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_pinterest_ads__advertiser_report
metadata.comment: Each record represents the daily performance of an Advertiser.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: The performance date of the record.

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

impressions.name: impressions
impressions.comment: The number of paid and earned impressions that occurred on the day of the record.

clicks.name: clicks
clicks.comment: The number of paid and earned clicks that occurred on the day of the record.

spend.name: spend
spend.comment: The amount of spend that occurred on the day of the record.

total_conversions.name: total_conversions
total_conversions.comment: This is the sum of all website conversions, otherwise known as the number of conversion events. For example, if you track sign-ups and checkouts on your website, it's the sum of all sign-ups and checkouts attributed from clicks, engagements, and views on Pinterest. Example: If a user clicks on a Pinterest ad and completes two different conversion actions (e.g., signs up and checks out), this would be counted as 2 total conversions.

total_conversions_quantity.name: total_conversions_quantity
total_conversions_quantity.comment: Refers to the total count of items or units involved in the conversions. This metric is often more granular and relates to the specific quantity of products or services purchased or actions taken. Example: If a single checkout includes three items, the total_conversions_quantity would be 3, even though it counts as one total_conversion.

total_conversions_value.name: total_conversions_value
total_conversions_value.comment: Total conversions order value. The total value of the conversions, calculated by summing up the revenue or order values attributed to conversions. Converted from the source `total_conversions_value_in_micro_dollar` field.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_pinterest_ads__campaign_history
metadata.comment: Each record represents a version of a Pinterest Campaign.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: The ID of the related Campaign.

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

default_ad_group_budget_in_micro_currenc.name: default_ad_group_budget_in_micro_currency
default_ad_group_budget_in_micro_currenc.comment: When transitioning from campaign budget optimization to non-campaign budget optimization, the default_ad_group_budget_in_micro_currency will propagate to each child ad groups daily budget. Unit is micro currency of the associated advertiser account.

is_automated_campaign.name: is_automated_campaign
is_automated_campaign.comment: Specifies whether the campaign was created in the automated campaign flow

is_campaign_budget_optimization.name: is_campaign_budget_optimization
is_campaign_budget_optimization.comment: Determines if a campaign automatically generate ad-group level budgets given a campaign budget to maximize campaign outcome. When transitioning from non-cbo to cbo, all previous child ad group budget will be cleared.

is_flexible_daily_budgets.name: is_flexible_daily_budgets
is_flexible_daily_budgets.comment: Determine if a campaign has flexible daily budgets setup.

campaign_status.name: campaign_status
campaign_status.comment: The status of the Campaign. One of "ACTIVE", "ARCHIVED", "PAUSED"

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when a record was last synced.

start_time.name: start_time
start_time.comment: Timestamp when the campaign became active or was scheduled to start.

end_time.name: end_time
end_time.comment: Timestamp when the campaign ended or is scheduled to end.

budget_spend_cap.name: budget_spend_cap
budget_spend_cap.comment: Maximum amount allowed to be spent over a specified budget period for the campaign.

lifetime_spend_cap.name: lifetime_spend_cap
lifetime_spend_cap.comment: Total maximum spend allowed across the entire lifespan of the campaign.

objective_type.name: objective_type
objective_type.comment: The primary goal of the campaign (e.g., brand awareness, conversions, video views).

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Boolean representing whether the record is the most recent version of the object.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_pinterest_ads__pin_promotion_history
metadata.comment: Each record represents a version of a Pinterest Pin promotion.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

base_url.name: base_url
base_url.comment: The base URL of the ad, extracted from the `destination_url`.

url_host.name: url_host
url_host.comment: The URL host of the ad, extracted from the `destination_url`.

url_path.name: url_path
url_path.comment: The URL path of the ad, extracted from the `destination_url`.

utm_source.name: utm_source
utm_source.comment: The utm_source parameter of the ad, extracted from the `destination_url`.

utm_medium.name: utm_medium
utm_medium.comment: The utm_medium parameter of the ad, extracted from the `destination_url`.

utm_campaign.name: utm_campaign
utm_campaign.comment: The utm_campaign parameter of the ad, extracted from the `destination_url`.

utm_content.name: utm_content
utm_content.comment: The utm_content parameter of the ad, extracted from the `destination_url`.

utm_term.name: utm_term
utm_term.comment: The utm_term parameter of the ad, extracted from the `destination_url`.

pin_status.name: pin_status
pin_status.comment: The status of the Pin promotion. One of "ACTIVE", "ARCHIVED", "PAUSED"

creative_type.name: creative_type
creative_type.comment:
The creative type. 
One of "APP", "APP_VIDEO", "BOARD", "CAROUSEL", "CINEMATIC", "COMMERCE", "MAX_VIDEO", "NATIVE_VIDEO", "REGULAR", 
"SEARCH_PROMINENCE", "SEARCH_PROMINENCE_CAROUSEL", "SHOPPING", "SHOP_THE_PIN", "THIRD_PARTY", or "VIDEO".

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when a record was last synced.

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Boolean representing whether the record is the most recent version of the object.

metadata.name: stg_pinterest_ads__pin_promotion_targeting_report
metadata.comment: Contains performance metrics for promoted Pins segmented by applied targeting criteria, including targeting type, value, and related campaign metadata.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when a record was last synced.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: The performance date of the record.

targeting_type.name: targeting_type
targeting_type.comment: Reporting targeting type for ads. Possible values 'KEYWORD', 'APPTYPE', 'GENDER', 'LOCATION', 'PLACEMENT', 'COUNTRY', 'TARGETED_INTEREST', 'PINNER_INTEREST', 'AUDIENCE_INCLUDE', 'GEO', 'AGE_BUCKET', 'REGION', 'QUIZ_RESULT', 'AGE_BUCKET_AND_GENDER'

targeting_value.name: targeting_value
targeting_value.comment: The specific value applied within the targeting type (e.g., 'Fashion' for interest, 'New York' for location).

ad_group_id.name: ad_group_id
ad_group_id.comment: The ID of the related Ad group.

advertiser_id.name: advertiser_id
advertiser_id.comment: The ID of the related Advertiser.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id

spend.comment: The amount of spend that occurred on the day of the record.

total_conversions.comment: This is the sum of all website conversions, otherwise known as the number of conversion events. For example, if you track sign-ups and checkouts on your website, it's the sum of all sign-ups and checkouts attributed from clicks, engagements, and views on Pinterest. Example: If a user clicks on a Pinterest ad and completes two different conversion actions (e.g., signs up and checks out), this would be counted as 2 total conversions.

total_conversions_quantity.name: total_conversions_quantity
total_conversions_quantity.comment: Refers to the total count of items or units involved in the conversions. This metric is often more granular and relates to the specific quantity of products or services purchased or actions taken. Example: If a single checkout includes three items, the total_conversions_quantity would be 3, even though it counts as one total_conversion.
